metaTitle: Boiler Making Noise | What It Means and When to Call
metaDescription: Boiler making noise? Learn what banging, humming or gurgling sounds can mean and when to call a heating engineer.
h1: Why Is My Boiler Making Noise?
intro: If your boiler has started banging, humming, vibrating or gurgling, the noise is usually a sign that something in the boiler or heating system is no longer working normally.
safetyWarning: New or worsening boiler noises should be checked before they turn into a full breakdown.
symptoms: Noise that gets worse as the boiler heats up
safeChecks: Note when the noise happens during the heating cycle
Check the pressure gauge for obvious issues
Look for any visible drips or movement around exposed pipework
doNotDiy: Do not remove covers to investigate internal components
Do not attempt to bleed or adjust sealed boiler parts without guidance
Do not ignore loud noises that are getting worse
typicalFixes: Correcting component faults linked to overheating or vibration

sections.heading: What boiler noise can point to
sections.body: Unusual noise can come from poor circulation, pressure issues, wear in internal parts or water behaving badly inside the heat exchanger.

sections.heading: Why noises should not be dismissed
sections.body: Noisy operation is often the early warning sign of a fault rather than just an irritation. Left alone, the same issue can lead to lockouts, leaks or component failure.

sections.heading: When the noise needs an engineer
sections.body: If the boiler is suddenly loud, the sound is worsening or you are also seeing poor heating performance, book a repair visit to find the cause properly.
